coredata.dc:title: Electrochemiluminescence study of AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/chitosan nanocomposite modified carbon nanofiber screen-printed electrode with [Ru(bpy)3]2+/TPrA
coredata.dc:creator: Rizwan, Mohammad
Ahmed, Minhaz Uddin
coredata.dc:description: Generation of ECL by a set of luminophore-corectant system is highly dependent on the concentration of the luminophore, coreactant; applied photomultiplier tube (PMT) voltage; materials, dimensions and the surface area of the electrode. Therefore, in this work firstly concentration of the luminophore ([Ru(bpy)3]2+), coreactant (TPrA); applied voltage on PMT was optimised for the high ECL intensity with the carbon nanofiber screen-printed electrode (CNFs-SPE). Secondly, with the optimised concentration of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TPrA and optimised applied PMT voltage; ECL intensity of the bare poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) screen-printed electrode (PEDOT-SPE), carbon screen-printed electrode (C-SPE) and CNFs-SPE was compared. Study demonstrated that the ECL generated on the surface of the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE was 1214 (a.u.), 3779 (a.u.), and 6346 (a.u.) respectively. Thirdly, bare CNFs-SPE was modified with Chitosan, and nanocomposite of: SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, and AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan; and their ECL was compared. This study illustrated that AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE (AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE) displayed highest ECL (10,452 a.u.), and ~ 200% more as compare to the bare CNFs-SPE. Further, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE displayed ~ 200% increase in effective surface area and electronic conductivity in comparison to the bare CNFs-SPE. In the fourth and last study, ECL stability of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite for continuous 10 cycles was studied, which showed that AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE was highly stable with only ~ 10% decrease in ECL intensity.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Electrochemiluminescence
Nanocomposite
Luminophore
Coreactant: Photomultiplier tube
Carbon nanofiber screen-printed electrode

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Facile one-pot eco-friendly synthesis of nanocomposite
Effect of [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TrPA concentration and applied PMT voltage
Study of ECL on different electrodes
Comparative ECL intensity of the modified CNFs-SPE with the bare CNFs-SPE
Study of electronic conductivity and effective electrode surface area of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE
Study of effect of ECL cycles on stability of the ECL intensity on the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Optimization of the concentration of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TrPA, and PMT voltage: (A) ECL intensity of 0.1, 1 and 10 mM [Ru(bpy)3]2+ with 100 mM TPrA and PMT voltage 700 V at the bare CNFs-SPE; (B) ECL intensity of 1, 10, 100 and 1000 mM TPrA with 1 mM [Ru(bpy)3]2+ and PMT voltage 700 V at the bare CNFs-SPE; and (C) ECL intensity at 400, 500, 600 and 700 V applied PMT voltage with 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) and 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) and total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS at the bare CNFs-SPE.
Comparison of ECL and EIS of PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE: (A) ECL intensity curves; and (B) EIS, Nyquist plots.

Study of nanocomposite and ECL intensity comparison of the bare CNFs-SPE and the nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE: (A) Absorption spectra of the CdTe-QDs, AuNPs, Chitosan and AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan; (B) ECL intensity curve of the bare CNFs-SPE and AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE; and (C) ECL intensity peak bar diagram of the bare CNFs-SPE and various modified CNFs-SPEs: (a) bare CNFs-SPE (b) Chitosan- (c) SWCNTs/Chitosan- (d) CdTe-QDs/Chitosan- (e) AuNPs/Chitosan- (f) CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- (g) AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- (h) CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan-, and (i) AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- modified CNFs-SPE.
Electrochemical study of the bare CNFs-SPE and the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE: (A) CV curves; and (B) EIS, Nyquist plots.
Effect of ECL cycles on the stability of the ECL intensity of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE.
ECL of bare CNFs-SPE and AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE with [Ru(bpy)3]2+- TrPA system
Mechanism of ECL generation due to [Ru(bpy)3]2+ /TPrA system
Mechanism of ECL generation due to CdTe/TPrA system

head.ce:title: Electrochemiluminescence study of AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/chitosan nanocomposite modified carbon nanofiber screen-printed electrode with [Ru(bpy)3]2+/TPrA

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Biosensors and Biotechnology Laboratory, Faculty of Science, Universiti Brunei Darussalam, Jalan Tungku Link, Gadong BE 1410, Brunei Darussalam
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Biosensors and Biotechnology Laboratory, Faculty of Science, Universiti Brunei Darussalam, Jalan Tungku Link, Gadong, BE 1410, Brunei Darussalam

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Natural Sciences, Bangor University, Bangor, Gwynedd, LL57 2DG, Wales, United Kingdom
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Natural Sciences, Bangor University, Bangor, Gwynedd, LL57 2DG, Wales, United Kingdom

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Generation of ECL by a set of luminophore-corectant system is highly dependent on the concentration of the luminophore, coreactant; applied photomultiplier tube (PMT) voltage; materials, dimensions and the surface area of the electrode. Therefore, in this work firstly concentration of the luminophore ([Ru(bpy)3]2+), coreactant (TPrA); applied voltage on PMT was optimised for the high ECL intensity with the carbon nanofiber screen-printed electrode (CNFs-SPE). Secondly, with the optimised concentration of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TPrA and optimised applied PMT voltage; ECL intensity of the bare poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) screen-printed electrode (PEDOT-SPE), carbon screen-printed electrode (C-SPE) and CNFs-SPE was compared. Study demonstrated that the ECL generated on the surface of the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE was 1214 (a.u.), 3779 (a.u.), and 6346 (a.u.) respectively. Thirdly, bare CNFs-SPE was modified with Chitosan, and nanocomposite of: SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, and AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan; and their ECL was compared. This study illustrated that AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE (AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE) displayed highest ECL (10,452 a.u.), and ~ 200% more as compare to the bare CNFs-SPE. Further, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE displayed ~ 200% increase in effective surface area and electronic conductivity in comparison to the bare CNFs-SPE. In the fourth and last study, ECL stability of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite for continuous 10 cycles was studied, which showed that AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan/CNFs-SPE was highly stable with only ~ 10% decrease in ECL intensity.

ce:section.ce:para: The electrogenerated chemiluminescence or electrochemiluminescence (ECL) is an electrochemical phenomenon in which light is emitted without emission of the heat when electron transfer take place between the electrogenerated species in the presence of high applied voltage [1]. Currently, ECL has become an important and powerful analytical technology in many fields. It is routinely employed in biomedical [2] and environmental application [3] for the fabrication of the ECL based biosensor due to its high sensitivity and versatility, low background signal and simple optical setup [4]. This allow scientist good temporal and spatial control. Therefore, various sets of luminophore-coreactant systems are being developed as a source of ECL signals as well as being studied with various electrodes: such as, tris(2,2′-bipyridyl)‑ruthenium(II) [Ru(bpy)3]2+- tri-n-propylamine (TPrA) [4], luminol-H2O2 [5], luminol-TPrA [6], [Ru(bpy)3]2+-N-(3-aminopropyl) diethanolamine [7], luminol-N-Hydroxysuccinimide [8], artemisinin-luminol [9] and lucigenin-thiourea dioxide [10] has been studied on different bare electrodes [4–10] or electrodes modified with various types of nanocomposite [2,3,11–13].

Among different sets of the reported luminophore-coreactant systems, [Ru(bpy)3]2+- TPrA system is much popular among scientist across the globe for various practical applications due to stable ECL signal, easy to work on physiological pH, minimal toxic effect on the biological samples. As, ECL intensity is highly dependent on the materials, surface area and dimensions of the electrode as well as the applied photomultiplier tube (PMT) voltage; concentration of the luminophore ([Ru(bpy)3]2+) and coreactant (TrPA) [4,5,14,15]. However, as per literature survey and best of our knowledge there are no study reported on the bare carbon nanofiber screen-printed electrode (CNFs-SPE) with the optimised PMT voltage; concentration of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+ and TrPA for high ECL; ECL of the bare CNFs-SPE relative to the bare poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) screen-printed electrode (PEDOT-SPE) and the bare carbon screen-printed electrode (C-SPE); the ECL of the bare CNFs-SPE and the relative ECL of the CNFs-SPE modified with Chitosan, and nanocomposite of: SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan; and the ECL stability study at the nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE with [Ru(bpy)3]2+- TPrA system for continuous several cycles.

Therefore, in this work we have studied the effect and optimised: (a) the applied PMT voltage; (b) concentration of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+, and (c) concentration of the TrPA for high ECL; (d) studied and compared the ECL of bare CNFs-SPE, bare PEDOT-SPE and bare C-SPE; (e) the ECL of bare CNFs-SPE and CNFs-SPE modified with Chitosan, and nanocomposite of: SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan; and (f) the ECL stability study at the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE with [Ru(bpy)3]2+- TPrA system for continuous 10 cycles.

Tris(2,2′-bipyridyl)dichlororuthenium(II) hexahydrate ([Ru(bpy)3]Cl2.6H2O) and tripropylamine (TPrA), disodium phosphate (Na2HPO4), monosodium phosphate (NaH2PO4.H2O), CdTe-QDs coated with ligand carboxyl group having size 2–10 nm, potassium ferrocyanide, and potassium ferricyanide were all purchased from Sigma-Aldrich (Saint Louis, USA). Chitosan (85% deacetylated) was purchased from Alfa Aesar (Ward Hill, M.A). Colloidal solutions of gold nanoparticles suspended in H2O without any preservative having size 10 nm were purchased from BBI Solutions (Cardiff, UK). SWCNTs with a specific surface BET >1000 m2 g−1, diameter of 2 nm and length of several microns were purchased from DropSens (Asturias, Spain). The ECL probe and redox couple solutions were prepared in-house. 10 mM PBS-buffer pH 7.4 was used throughout the experiment for preparation of [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TrPA, redox couple [Fe(CN)6]3/4- for the ECL and electrochemical analysis respectively. All chemicals and reagents were of ≥95% purity and used as received. In addition, all the solutions were freshly prepared in Milli-Q water (deionised with specific resistance ~ 18 MΩ cm−1).

For the purpose of ECL measurement, MPI-A Capillary Electrophoresis Electrochemiluminescence Analyser system was purchased from Xi'an Yima Opto-Electrical Technology Co., Ltd. (Xian, Shaanxi, Mainland China). An in-house working ECL cell was used to receive the light emitted from the ECL reactions and to conduct it to an ultra-sensitive single photon-counting module or photomultiplier tube (PMT) connected to MPI-A software. The ECL cell was made up of tubular transparent glass tube (diameter 1.5 cm and height 5 cm) completely wrapped with silver-mirror film, leaving only the base uncovered to allow the transmission of the ECL light to the PMT. The ECL cell was placed over the PMT to obtain the ECL signal in a dark box. Electrodes containing [Ru(bpy)3]Cl2-TPrA was immersed in the ECL cell and mounted over PMT to receive the ECL intensity signal. In order to electrochemical study: electrical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and cyclic voltammetry (CV) was performed with an Autolab PGSTAT101 III potentiostat/galvanostat (Metrohm, Netherlands) in combination with the Autolab Nova 1.10 software. Absorption spectra was recorded using Implen NanoPhotometer (CA, USA). The disposable working CNFs-SPE, PEDOT-SPE and C-SPE on ceramic substrate with dimension L33 × W10 × H0.5 mm was procured from DropSens (Asturias, Spain). CNFs-SPE, PEDOT-SPE and C-SPE consist of a carbon counter electrode, a silver reference electrode and a 4-mm diameter working electrode, with a maximum working volume of 50 μL. All study was carried out at RT and atmospheric pressure (21 ± 1 °C, air-conditioned laboratory). All experimental points are an average of three replicates (n = 3) and error bars represents the standard deviation of the three study obtained with three electrodes.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Facile one-pot eco-friendly synthesis of nanocomposite
ce:section.ce:para: Chitosan solution (1 μg mL−1) was prepared in 1% acetic acid and was sonicated for few min to allow it to be mixed properly. 100 μg mL−1 stock solution of SWCNTs was prepared in a glass vial and was sonicated for 12 h at RT. The SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan was synthesized in one-pot using different weight ratio of: CdTe-QDs (10 μg mL−1), AuNPs (20 μg mL−1), SWCNTs (10 μg mL−1), and Chitosan (1 μg mL−1) mixed in the same volume ratio, stirred for 6 h and ultrasonicated for 2 h to allow it to be mixed and stored at 4 °C when not in use.

Since the electrode materials, its surface modification or dimensions affect the ECL intensity, therefore SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan was used to modify the CNFs-SPE [5,14,15]. Therefore, 10 μL of SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan was dropped onto CNFs-SPE surface to bound via chemisorption [16], dried at RT for an hour and washed with water to remove any loose nanocomposite followed by drying using N2; their ECL was studied and compared. Among all the nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE demonstrated highest ECL as illustrated in Scheme 1 .

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TrPA concentration and applied PMT voltage
ce:section.ce:para: The ECL intensity is highly dependable on the concentration of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TrPA and applied PMT voltage, therefore to optimize the [Ru(bpy)3]2+ concentration for the high ECL intensity on the surface of the bare CNFs-SPE, three concentrations (0.1, 1 and 10 mM) of [Ru(bpy)3]2+ was studied with 100 mM TPrA, each 1 mL with cell volume maintained to 4 mL using PBS and PMT voltage 700 V. This study demonstrated that the 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) and 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) with the total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS give highest ECL intensity at PMT voltage 700 V as shown in Fig. 1A. To optimize the concentration of the TPrA, four concentrations (1, 10, 100 and 1000 mM) of TPrA was studied with 1 mM [Ru(bpy)3]2+ each 1 mL with cell volume maintained to 4 mL using PBS and PMT voltage 700 V. This study demonstrated that the 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) and 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) with the total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS give highest ECL intensity at PMT voltage 700 V as shown in Fig. 1B. Whereas, different PMT voltage (400, 500, 600 and 700 V) was used to study high ECL at the bare CNFs-SPE with 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) and 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) with total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS it was observed 700 V an optimum voltage for the high ECL as shown in Fig. 1C. All the study was conducted at the ECL following ECL parameters: initial voltage 0.2 V, high voltage 1.25 V, low voltage 0.2 V, scan rate 100 mVs−1, amplifying series 2, sensitivity (A/V) 1.e−002. This study shows as the concentration of the luminophore ([Ru(bpy)3]2+), concentration of the corearctant (TPrA) and the applied PMT voltage radically changes ECL intensity at the electrode of the same materials.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study of ECL on different electrodes
ce:section.ce:para: Further ECL study was performed on the at the bare PEDOT-SPE and C-SPE with 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) and 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) with total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS at PMT voltage 700 V, at ECL parameters: initial voltage 0.2 V, high voltage 1.25 V, low voltage 0.2 V, scan rate 100 mVs−1, amplifying series 2, sensitivity (A/V) 1.e−002. Observed ECL intensity curve of the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE are shown in Fig. 2A. The Fig. 2A demonstrate that changing the electrode materials drastically change the ECL intensity without changing the concentration and volume of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mL, 1 mM) and TPrA (1 mL, 100 mM) and applied PMT voltage. Observed ECL intensity peak for the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE was in the order of 1214 (a.u.), 3779 (a.u.), and 6346 (a.u.) respectively. This could be inferred as: either increase in electronic conductivity or surface area or increase in both electronic conductivity and surface area increases the ECL intensity, if all other parameters are constant. The working electrodes surface of the electrodes: PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs- SPE are respectively made up of PEDOT, Carbon and CNFs. The electronic conductivity of the PEDOT, carbon and CNFs are in the order of PEDOT < Carbon < CNFs, which shows high electronic conductivity for the CNFs due to both highly conductive nature of the CNFs-SPE and large surface area as well as characteristic edge plane defect which additionally facilitates rapid electron transfer [17]. Further electronic conductivity of the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs- SPE was studied using EIS between 100 kHz and 0.1 Hz at number of frequency 50 Hz ac amplitude of 5 mV rms with redox couple (5 mM) prepared in 0.1 M KCl solution. The EIS study (Fig. 2B) demonstrated that the electron transfer resistance (Rct) of the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs- SPE are in the order of PEDOT > Carbon > CNFs. Therefore, from Nyquist plots it could be inferred that the PEDOT is less conductive than the carbon, whereas the high electronic conductivity of the CNF due to both large surface area as well as characteristic edge plane dominate over the carbon electronic conductivity. This study demonstrated that the ECL intensity is highly dependent on the materials of the surface of the working electrode. Mechanism of the ECL generation due to [Ru(bpy)3]2+/TPrA system at the surface of the PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE is shown in Scheme 2 [18].

As, AuNPs shows a characteristic absorption peak at 520–530 nm [19]. Therefore, the synthesized nanocomposite was studied to demonstrate that the characteristic absorption peak is observed at 520–530 nm. The synthesized nanocomposite demonstrated the characteristic absorption peak at 520–530 nm as shown in Fig. 3A, which illustrate the successful formation of the nanocomposite.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Comparative ECL intensity of the modified CNFs-SPE with the bare CNFs-SPE
ce:section.ce:para: The CNFs-SPE was modified with Chitosan, and nanocomposite of SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan, AuNPs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan, CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan, and AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan; and their ECL intensity was studied with 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) and 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) with total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS at PMT voltage 700 V, at ECL parameters: initial voltage 0.2 V, high voltage 1.25 V, low voltage 0.2 V, scan rate 100 mVs−1, amplifying series 2, sensitivity (A/V) 1.e−002. Comparison of the ECL intensity of the bare CNFs-SPE and the CNFs-SPE modified with the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan are given in the Fig. 3B. Further, ECL intensity was studied at the Chitosan-, and SWCNTs/Chitosan-, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan-, AuNPs/Chitosan-, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- and CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan- nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE. The ECL intensities of the (a) bare CNFs-SPE (b) Chitosan- (c) SWCNTs/Chitosan- (d) CdTe-QDs/Chitosan- (e) AuNPs/Chitosan- (f) CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- (g) AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- (h) CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan- and (i) AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- modified CNFs-SPE are respectively shown in Fig. 3C (bars a-i). From, the Fig. 3C it could be inferred that the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE generate highest ECL among: bare CNFs-SPE, and CNFs-SPE modified with Chitosan- and nanocomposite of: SWCNTs/Chitosan-, CdTe-QDs/Chitosan-, AuNPs/Chitosan-, CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-, AuNPs/SWCNTs/Chitosan- and CdTe-QDs/AuNPs/Chitosan. The ECL intensity generated from the surface of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE was about 200% more than the bare CNFs-SPE. This high ECL could be due change in the materials of the working electrode comprising the different nanomaterials: AuNPs [20], CdTe-QDs [21], SWCNTs [22] and CNFs [17]. Among these the large surface area and high conductivity of AuNPs, luminophore and electrical properties of the CdTe-QDs, the presence of large aspect ratio and larger surface area of SWCNTs, and large surface area as well as characteristic edge plane of CNFs might be synergistically involved in enhancing the ECL at the modified electrodes. While the nanocomposite containing the CdTe-QDs coated with ligand carboxyl group could be supporting the fast diffusion of the [Ru(bpy)3]2+ on the modified electrodes, modified with the nanocomposite containing CdTe-QDs due to the attractive electrostatic interaction between the positively charged [Ru(bpy)3]2+ and the negatively charged carboxyl group of the coated CdTe-QDs. The generation of ECL at the bare CNFs-SPE could be due to the [Ru(bpy)3]2+/TPrA system as explained in the Scheme 2 [18]. While generation of the ECL at the surface of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE could be due to the [Ru(bpy)3]2+/TPrA system as explained in the Scheme 2 [18], as well as the ECL generated by the CdTe-QDs/TPrA system [21]. The mechanism of the ECL generation due to CdTe-QDs/TPrA system at the surface of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE is shown in Scheme 3 [21].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study of electronic conductivity and effective electrode surface area of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE
ce:section.ce:para:
Further, cyclic voltammetry (CV) was performed in a potential range from −0.4 to 0.7 V at a scan rate of 100 mVs−1 with the redox couple (5 mM) prepared in 0.1 M KCl solution to study the electronic conductivity and the surface area of the bare CNFs-SPE and the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE as shown in Fig. 4A. The Fig. 4A shows that the electronic conductivity of the bare CNFs-SPE was much lower than the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNTs/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE. This could be inferred as the nanomaterials (AuNPs, SWCNTs, CdTe-QDs) in the nanocomposite (AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan) increase the electronic conductivity. Based on the peak of the oxidative electronic current obtained in the CV, the Randles-Servcik Eq. (i) was employed to determine the effective electrode area of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE in comparison to the bare CNFs-SPE [23].
(i)
i p
= 2.69 ×
10 5
n
3 / 2
AD
1 / 2
V
1 / 2
C o
where, ip = highest oxidative electronic current, n = number of electrons, A = effective electrode area, D = diffusion coefficient 6.7 × 10−6 (cm2 S −1), V = scan rate (Vs−1) and Co = concentration (mol cm−3); using the value of all the parameters in Eq. (i) the effective electrode area of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-modified CNFs-SPE in comparison of the bare CNFs-SPE could be calculated. It shows that the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE had ~ 200% more effective surface area as compared to the bare CNFs-SPE and demonstrated ~ 200% more increase in electronic current, which could be due to the cumulative surface area and the electronic property of the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite bearing AuNPs, CdTe-QDs and SWCNTs on the CNFs-SPE. Further, EIS study was performed for the interface impedance behaviour of the bare CNFs-SPE and the CNFs-SPE modified with the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite between 100 kHz and 0.1 Hz at number of frequency 50 Hz ac amplitude of 5 mV rms with redox couple (5 mM) prepared in 0.1 M KCl solution as shown in Fig. 4B. Form Fig. 4B, it could be inferred that the Rct of the Nyquist plot of AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-modified CNFs-SPE is lesser than Nyquist plot of bare CNFs-SPE. This suggest that AuNPs, CdTe-QDs, SWCNTs contribute in decreasing the impedance on the surface of AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-modified CNFs-SPE.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study of effect of ECL cycles on stability of the ECL intensity on the AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE
ce:section.ce:para: As the study demonstrated that AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE showed ~ 200% increase in the ECL, and both ~ 200% increase in electronic conductivity and the effective surface area in comparison to the bare CNFs-SPE. Therefore, further effect of ECL cycles on the stability of the ECL intensity was studied with 1 mL [Ru(bpy)3]2+ (1 mM) and 1 mL TPrA (100 mM) with total cell volume 4 mL maintained using PBS at PMT voltage 700 V, at ECL parameters: initial voltage 0.2 V, high voltage 1.25 V, low voltage 0.2 V, scan rate 100 mVs−1, amplifying series 2, sensitivity (A/V) 1.e−002 as shown in Fig. 5 . The Fig. 5 demonstrate that the curve of the first ECL cycle has peak height >10, 000 (a.u.), and the tenth ECL cycle has peak height >9000 (a.u.), which establishes that AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE is highly stable to work with only about 10% decrease in the ECL intensity even after the ten continuous ECL cycles. The slow decrease in ECL intensity could be due to gradual wash off the non-covalently bounded AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite from the CNFs-SPE immersed in the ECL cell.

For the first time in this study effect of the concentration of the luminophore ([Ru(bpy)3]2+), corearctant (TPrA) and the applied PMT voltage was studied for the high ECL intensity on the bare CNFs-SPE. This study demonstrated that the ECL intensity is highly dependent on the concentration of [Ru(bpy)3]2+, TPrA and applied PMT voltage. Further, with the optimised concentration of luminophore ([Ru(bpy)3]2+), co-reactant (TPrA) and applied PMT voltage, effect of the ECL generation on three different electrodes with working electrodes made up of three different materials (PEDOT-SPE, C-SPE and CNFs-SPE) was studied. This study demonstrated that generation of the ECL intensity is highly dependent on the materials of the working electrodes, the working electrodes which are made up of conductive materials or have high effective surface area or made up of the materials having both highly conductive materials and high effective surface area generate high ECL intensity. Therefore, our study demonstrated PEDOT-SPE < C-SPE < CNFs-SPE order of increase in ECL intensity among the electrode with working electrode made up of PEDOT, carbon and CNFs. As our study illustrated that the electrode made up of nanomaterials (CNFs) showed highest ECL intensity. So, in our further study we also modified the bare CNFs-SPE with chitosan and different nanocomposite and compared the generation of ECL, which exhibited that among different nanocomposite, CNFs-SPE modified with AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite generate ~ 200% more ECL than the bare CNFs-SPE with highly stable ECL signal. Additionally, AuNPs/CdTe-QDs/SWCNT/Chitosan-nanocomposite modified CNFs-SPE showed ~ 200% increase in both electronic conductivity and effective surface area.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: Mohammad Rizwan wishes to thank the UBD's Graduate Research Scholarship (GRS) for his Ph.D. fellowship. This work was partly supported by the Brunei Research Council [Grant# BRC-10] of Negara Brunei Darussalam.
